anchor.snippet: Subtraction is an operation that represents removal of objects from a collection
subtraction is the inverse of addition
Subtraction is anti-commutative, meaning that changing the order changes the sign of the answer
It is also not associative , meaning that when one subtracts more than two numbers, the order in which subtraction is performed matters
Because 0 is the additive identity , subtraction of it does not change a number
The number being subtracted is the subtrahend , while the number it is subtracted from is the minuend
Imagine a line segment of length b with the left end labeled a and the right end labeled c
it takes 4 steps to the left from 3 to get to
Subtraction of natural numbers is not closed
The subtraction of a real number (the subtrahend) from another (the minuend) can then be defined as the addition of the minuend and the additive inverse of the subtrahend
Subtraction is anti-commutative , meaning that if one reverses the terms in a difference left-to-right, the result is the negative of the original result
Subtraction is non-associative , which comes up when one tries to define repeated subtraction
the integer ( a − 1) is the largest integer less than a , also known as the predecessor of a
When subtracting two numbers with units of measurement such as kilograms or pounds , they must have the same unit

annotations.label: Percentage change vs. percentage point change

anchor.snippet: Percentage change represents the relative change between the two quantities as a percentage, while percentage point change is simply the number obtained by subtracting the two percentages
suppose that 30% of widgets made in a factory are defective
The method of complements is a technique used to subtract one number from another using only the addition of positive numbers
the ones' complement of y is added to x and one is added to the sum
The minuend is 704, the subtrahend is 512
The required sum (5) is too small

annotations.label: Subtraction from left to right example

anchor.snippet: This result is only penciled in
each digit of the subtrahend is subtracted from the digit above it starting from right to left
A variant of the American method where all borrowing is done before all subtraction
The partial differences method is different from other vertical subtraction methods because no borrowing or carrying takes place
one can count up the numbers between the subtrahend and the minuend
to split up the subtraction into small steps
The same change method uses the fact that adding or subtracting the same number from the minuend and subtrahend does not change the answer
